title: PRD-015: Config Validation and Remote Loading

overview.description: Add configuration validation and remote config loading to help teams share and validate jarvy.toml configurations.
goals: Validate jarvy.toml before running setup to catch errors early
Provide helpful error messages with suggestions for typos
Load configs from HTTP(S) URLs for team sharing
Support authenticated requests for private configs

userStories.title: Config Validation
userStories.description: As a config author, I want to validate my jarvy.toml before sharing so that team members don't encounter errors.
userStories.notes: Fully implemented in src/commands/validate.rs (537 lines) with 6 unit tests

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: TOML syntax errors show line numbers
acceptanceCriteria.notes: Uses toml crate error formatting with line/column info
Checks against registered tools in registry
Uses strsim crate with Jaro-Winkler algorithm (0.85 threshold)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Hooks referencing unknown tools produce warnings
Exit code 0 for valid, non-zero for errors
acceptanceCriteria.notes: Uses output::ExitCode enum for proper exit codes

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Validation completes in <100ms for local files

userStories.title: Remote Config Loading
userStories.description: As a team lead, I want to host our team's jarvy.toml on a server so that all team members use the same environment configuration.
userStories.notes: Fully implemented with --header flag, validate --from, size limits, and 11 integration tests

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: jarvy setup --from <URL> fetches and uses remote config
HTTPS URLs work with valid certificates
acceptanceCriteria.notes: Uses ureq crate with native TLS

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: HTTP URLs rejected in release builds
acceptanceCriteria.notes: HTTPS required, HTTP blocked in release builds

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: --header flag allows custom headers for auth
acceptanceCriteria.notes: Implemented with clap::ArgAction::Append for multiple headers

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Clear error messages for network failures
acceptanceCriteria.notes: Proper error handling with user-friendly messages

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Size limit enforced (1MB) to prevent memory exhaustion
acceptanceCriteria.notes: Implemented with Content-Length check and limited reader (MAX_REMOTE_CONFIG_SIZE = 1MB)
Remote configs are parsed and validated before setup
Implemented with --from and --header flags on validate command

functionalRequirements.description: jarvy validate command checks local jarvy.toml
Unknown tool names detected with fuzzy matching suggestions
TOML syntax errors include line numbers
jarvy setup --from URL fetches remote configs
HTTPS enforced for remote configs (HTTP only in debug)
Custom headers supported for authenticated requests
functionalRequirements.notes: Implemented via --header flag on setup and validate commands

nonGoals: Config caching (fetch fresh each time) - CHANGED: Caching IS now implemented in ~/.jarvy/cache/configs/
Lock files (version pinning) - Moved to PRD-024

technicalConsiderations.details: Uses strsim crate with Jaro-Winkler algorithm (0.85 threshold)
Uses ureq crate (lighter than reqwest) with native TLS
technicalConsiderations.notes: Changed from reqwest to ureq for smaller binary size

technicalConsiderations.details: Tools registered via registry::REGISTRY with list_registered_tools()
No arbitrary code execution from configs; only declarative tool specs
Remote configs cached in ~/.jarvy/cache/configs/ with SHA256 URL hash as key

filesCreated.notes: Full validation implementation with 6 unit tests

filesModified.notes: Added validate command and --from flag to setup

dependencies.purpose: HTTP client for remote config fetching

implementationSteps.description: Create src/validate/mod.rs with ValidationResult, ValidationError structs
Implement known-tools registry check (expose tool names from registry)
Add fuzzy matching for tool name suggestions using strsim
Implement validate_config() function with all checks
implementationSteps.notes: Full implementation with tool, version, hook validation

implementationSteps.description: Add jarvy validate CLI command with --config and --strict flags

implementationSteps.notes: Implemented in main.rs and config loading flow

implementationSteps.description: Add reqwest dependency with minimal features
implementationSteps.notes: Used ureq instead for smaller binary

implementationSteps.description: Implement remote config fetching with HTTPS validation
Add --from and --header flags to jarvy setup command
implementationSteps.notes: Both --from and --header implemented with multiple header support

implementationSteps.description: Add --from flag to jarvy validate command
Write tests for remote config fetching
implementationSteps.notes: 11 tests in tests/remote_config.rs covering CLI args, validation, and error handling

completedWork.notes: Implemented with clap::ArgAction::Append for multiple headers

completedWork.item: Add size limit for remote configs
completedWork.notes: Enforce 1MB limit (MAX_REMOTE_CONFIG_SIZE) with Content-Length check and limited reader
11 tests in tests/remote_config.rs covering CLI args, validation, and error handling

risks.mitigation: Clear error messages; users can fall back to local config
HTTPS only, validation before use, no code execution
Used ureq instead of reqwest for smaller binary
